description: For a person or other organism to have and continue a life. A location phrase says where that life is lived, so 'mia mua womu liona' means I live at home. 'menui' (dwell) describes habitation; 'liona' keeps the living subject in view.
articulatory_notes: Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for 'l' and release into high-front 'i'. Keep stressed rounded middle-height back 'o' as a separate syllable. Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for dental 'n', carry the voice through the nose, and finish on open central 'a'.
sound_symbolism: The lateral and nasal keep the voice continuous through three changing vowels. Nothing snaps shut; the word carries on.
usage_notes: Location, duration, and manner can accompany this intransitive verb. Its event noun refers to living as an activity or way of life, as in 'liona ruela' (way of living), while 'lioru' names life as a whole and 'lima' states that an organism is alive.

semantic_domains.nature: the ongoing life of a person or other organism
semantic_domains.physical: biological living as an activity or condition
semantic_domains.spatial: living in a stated home, habitat, or other place
semantic_domains.temporal: a life continuing through a stated span
